articles.title: Heat stroke ward set up in Koppal - The Hans India
articles.search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Karnataka
articles.full_text:
Bengaluru:District administrations have begun strengthening healthcare infrastructure. In Koppal, a high-tech “heat stroke ward” has been set up at the district hospital. The facility includes 10 ICU-style beds, along with dedicated doctors and nursing staff to manage emergency cases. Hospital authorities stated that the move comes amid rising temperatures ranging between 40°C and 41°C in the region. The special ward is equipped to provide immediate treatment to patients suffering from heat exhaustion and heat stroke.
Doctors have also issued public guidelines, advising residents to avoid stepping out after 10 am unless necessary, drink water frequently, and seek medical attention if symptoms like dizziness, fatigue, or fainting occur. The situation has sparked concern among citizens, with authorities urging strict adherence to safety measures as temperatures continue to rise across the state.
